steps.title: Find the key contrast
steps.content:
The underlined lines create a stark contrast: The bleak fields are asleep, but My heart alone wakes. The word “alone” is decisive—it emphasizes that while the entire evening world rests, the speaker stands apart in wakefulness.

This is the defining emotional signal of the opening.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly captures what these lines do: they establish the speaker's sense of isolation while experiencing the evening. The contrast between the sleeping world and the speaker's solitary wakefulness creates loneliness within the broader evening scene.

The other choices either introduce comparisons not made (B—fields vs. water preference), add elements outside the text (C—other people's opinions), or reverse the relationship (D—suggests the speaker woke from sleep, when the passage shows the speaker awake while the world sleeps).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
